subject: Mathematics
title: AND, OR, NOT: Logical Connectives
description: One-minute skill: AND, OR, NOT: Logical Connectives.

hook.text: Combine simple statements with AND, OR, and NOT—and you can express any logical idea.

buildup.text: 'It's raining AND cold' is true only when both parts are true. One false part makes the whole false.

discovery.text: 'It's raining OR cold' is true when at least one part is true. Both false makes the whole false.

twist.text: NOT simply flips the truth value. 'NOT raining' is true exactly when 'raining' is false.

climax.text: These three connectives are logically complete—they can express any Boolean function whatsoever.

punchline.text: Three tiny words build every logical statement ever made.

quiz.question: When is 'A AND B' true?
options: Only when both A and B are true
When either A or B is true
When A is true and B is false
